subject: Chemistry
title: Nuclear Fission: Splitting the Atom
description: A fast breakdown of Nuclear Fission: Splitting the Atom for builders.

hook.text: In 1938, Otto Hahn split a uranium atom and released a million times more energy than any chemical reaction.

buildup.text: A neutron strikes uranium-235, making it unstable. The nucleus splits into two smaller atoms plus 2–3 neutrons.

discovery.text: Those freed neutrons hit more uranium atoms, causing a chain reaction. Each split releases enormous energy from E = mc².

twist.text: Lise Meitner actually explained the physics of fission. Hahn got the Nobel Prize alone—one of science's greatest injustices.

climax.text: Controlled fission powers nuclear reactors. Uncontrolled fission is an atomic bomb. Same reaction, different speeds.

punchline.text: Split one atom, free neutrons split more. A chain reaction of pure energy.

quiz.question: What sustains a nuclear fission chain reaction?
options: Neutrons released from each split strike more atoms, causing further splits
Heat from the first split melts surrounding atoms
Electrons escaping from split atoms trigger nearby nuclei
